gate_to_review: Gate F: Provider Adapter Kernel Activation
evidence_that_need_exists: Gate E passed repo-local operational validation with three live localhost iterations.
Product architecture requires provider adapters to normalize Codex, Claude, API, and future worker outputs into runner facts.
Phase 1B used only an API-compatible local stub; the next narrow slice must make that provider evidence contract executable and reusable before real providers are attached.

review_focus: confirm the first implementation slice stays local and stub-backed
confirm paid provider calls remain forbidden until a separate provider/cost activation decision
confirm subscription CLI paths are deferred

new_risks.summary: Parser may accept malformed or drifted provider output.
new_risks.mitigation: forced-drift fixtures must block; raw output digest and parser schema version are mandatory.

new_risks.summary: Provider adapter could hide cost-bearing behavior behind a local-looking path.
new_risks.mitigation: cost facts are required even when expected and actual USD are zero; unknown cost status blocks.

new_risks.summary: Subscription CLI or SDK paths may be tempting as cost-reduction shortcuts.
new_risks.mitigation: subscription_cli and subscription_sdk remain out of scope for the first slice.

new_risks.summary: Provider workers could be treated as authorizers instead of executors.
new_risks.mitigation: provider output cannot contain approval fields and cannot bypass task proposal or verifier decisions.

rollback_plan: disable provider-adapter-kernel task by reverting Gate F to pending or fail
ignore provider evidence emitted after the rollback decision
keep existing Phase 1B local stub evidence as the fallback baseline
do not delete existing append-only evidence records

minimal_first_slice.goal: Implement a repo-local provider adapter kernel that converts API-compatible stub output into normalized provider evidence without calling real paid providers.
scope_out: Codex CLI or Claude Code CLI daemonization
required_checks: uv run ruff check runtime_engine bin tests
expected_artifacts: normalized provider output with raw digest/ref

provider_evidence_requirements.raw_output_rule: Raw output must be stored before parsing when feasible; indexes store refs, digest, size, and storage class.
provider_evidence_requirements.drift_rule: Any parse failure, missing required field, unknown version, unexpected shape, or provider warning sets drift_detected=true.
provider_evidence_requirements.authorization_rule: Drifted output cannot authorize dispatch, approval, ratchet widening, release, or evidence promotion.

task_proposal.goal: Build the local provider adapter kernel using stubbed API-compatible output.
task_proposal.rationale: Gate E proved the local kernel can be operationally validated; the next bottleneck is a reusable provider adapter evidence contract before real providers are attached.

reasons: no external repo mutation or production side effect is requested

acceptance_criteria_for_activation: Gate F review confirms the proposal remains bounded to local/API-compatible provider work.
Gate F review confirms no real paid provider calls or subscription CLI daemonization are authorized.
Gate F review confirms the first implementation slice preserves raw+parsed provider evidence and drift detection.
Gate F review confirms expected_cost_usd is zero and cost facts are still required.
activation_exit_criteria: provider adapter kernel emits normalized provider evidence for an ok fixture
provider adapter kernel marks a forced-drift fixture as drift_detected=true
verifier blocks drifted provider evidence from authorization-sensitive use
local operational validation can include provider adapter evidence without contacting real providers
